title: Podman hangs on build
body:
Dupe of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17628 which is closed without resolution.

Dockerfile
```Dockerfile
# Start from the latest Ubuntu image
FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install required dependencies and .NET SDK
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y dotnet-sdk-8.0
RUN dotnet tool install --global dotnet-dump
```

`podman build --log-level debug -t example .`
```
time="2024-10-31T14:19:31+11:00" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2024-10-31T14:19:31+11:00" level=debug msg="Called build.PersistentPreRunE(podman build --log-level debug -t example .)"
time="2024-10-31T14:19:31+11:00" level=debug msg="SSH Ident Key \"C:\\\\Users\\\\coenraad.stijne\\\\.local\\\\share\\\\containers\\\\podman\\\\machine\\\\machine\" SHA256:V80r6QgNxmafGHg9JINaP+Ww53MH8ac7f58JNPzQ/nA ssh-ed25519"
time="2024-10-31T14:19:31+11:00" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.2.5/libpod/_ping"
time="2024-10-31T14:19:31+11:00" level=debug msg="Loading registries configuration \"/etc/containers/registries.conf\""
time="2024-10-31T14:19:31+11:00" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v5.2.5/libpod/build"
```

comments.body:
> Dupe of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17628 which is closed without resolution.

Did you read the issue? This is caused by a big context directory that must be copied into the VM. So if you change to a different small directory it should work nuch faster unless there is actual hang somewhere?
Also https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21627 so closing this as dup
